pokemon:pichu.name: Pichu
pokemon:pichu.summary: When Pichu plays with others, it may short out electricity with another Pichu, creating a shower of sparks. In that event, this Pokémon will begin crying, startled by the flash of sparks.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: When Pichu plays with others, it may short out electricity with another Pichu, creating a shower of sparks. In that event, this Pokémon will begin crying, startled by the flash of sparks.
The electric sacs in its cheeks are small. If even a little electricity leaks, it becomes shocked.
The electric sacs in its cheeks are small. If even a little electricity leaks, it becomes shocked.
It is unskilled at storing electric power. Any kind of shock causes it to discharge energy spontaneously.
The electric pouches on its cheeks are still small. They cannot store much electricity yet.
It is still inept at retaining electricity. When it is startled, it discharges power accidentally. It gets better at holding power as it grows older.
Despite its small size, it can zap even adult humans. However, if it does so, it also surprises itself.
It is not yet skilled at storing electricity. It may send out a jolt if amused or startled.
It is not yet skilled at storing electricity. It may send out a jolt if amused or startled.
It is not yet skilled at storing electricity. It may send out a jolt if amused or startled.
Pichu stores electricity in the sacs on its cheeks but discharges it inadvertently when agitated or excited. Being yet immature, the Pokémon's handling of electricity is rather inept.
Despite this Pokémon’s cute appearance, those who want to live with one should prepare to be on the receiving end of its electric jolts.
Pichu charges itself with electricity more easily on days with thunderclouds or when the air is very dry. You can hear the crackling of static electricity coming off this Pokémon.
It plays with others by touching tails and setting off sparks. This appears to be a test of courage.
The electric sacs in its cheeks are small. If even a little electricity leaks, it becomes shocked.
PICHU charges itself with electricity more easily on days with thunderclouds or when the air is very dry. You can hear the crackling of static electricity coming off this POKéMON.
When PICHU plays with others, it may short out electricity with another PICHU, creating a shower of sparks. In that event, this POKéMON will begin crying, startled by the flash of sparks.
The electric sacs in its cheeks are small. If even a little electricity leaks, it becomes shocked.
Despite its small size, it can zap even adult humans. However, if it does so, it also surprises itself.
Despite its small size, it can zap even adult humans. However, if it does so, it also surprises itself.
It is not yet skilled at controlling electricity. If you take your eyes off it, it may shock itself.
Despite its small size, it can zap even adult humans. However, if it does so, it also surprises itself.
It has electric sacs in its cheeks. When they’re fully charged, Pichu plays very energetically.
It still can’t use electricity well. When it’s surprised or excited, it discharges electricity unintentionally.
The electric sacs in its cheeks are small. If even a little electricity leaks, it becomes shocked.
The electric sacs in its cheeks are small. If even a little electricity leaks, it becomes shocked.
It plays with others by touching tails and setting off sparks. This appears to be a test of courage.
It is not yet skilled at storing electricity. It may send out a jolt if amused or startled.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: When Pichu plays with others, it may short out electricity with another Pichu, creating a shower of sparks. In that event, this Pokémon will begin crying, startled by the flash of sparks.

pokemon:pikachu.name: Pikachu
pokemon:pikachu.summary: This Pokémon has electricity-storing pouches on its cheeks. These appear to become electrically charged during the night while Pikachu sleeps. It occasionally discharges electricity when it is dozy after waking up.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: This Pokémon has electricity-storing pouches on its cheeks. These appear to become electrically charged during the night while Pikachu sleeps. It occasionally discharges electricity when it is dozy after waking up.
It occasionally uses an electric shock to recharge a fellow Pikachu that is in a weakened state.
It occasionally uses an electric shock to recharge a fellow Pikachu that is in a weakened state.
When several of these POKéMON gather, their electricity could build and cause lightning storms.
When it is anger­ ed, it immediately discharges the energy stored in the pouches in its cheeks.
It lives in forests with others. It stores electricity in the pouches on its cheeks.
It stores electricity in the electric sacs on its cheeks. When it releases pent-up energy in a burst, the electric power is equal to a lightning bolt.
It has small electric sacs on both its cheeks. If threatened, it looses electric charges from the sacs.
This intelligent POKéMON roasts hard BERRIES with electricity to make them tender enough to eat.
This intelligent Pokémon roasts hard berries with electricity to make them tender enough to eat.
When several of these POKéMON gather, their electricity can build and cause lightning storms.
Possesses cheek sacs in which it stores electricity. This clever forest-dweller roasts tough berries with an electric shock before consuming them.
This forest-dwelling Pokémon stores electricity in its cheeks, so you’ll feel a tingly shock if you touch it.
This forest-dwelling Pokémon stores electricity in its cheeks, so you’ll feel a tingly shock if you touch it.
It’s in its nature to store electricity. It feels stressed now and then if it’s unable to fully discharge the electricity.
Whenever Pikachu comes across something new, it blasts it with a jolt of electricity. If you come across a blackened berry, it’s evidence that this Pokémon mistook the intensity of its charge.
If it looses crackling power from the electric pouches on its cheeks, it is being wary.
It occasionally uses an electric shock to recharge a fellow PIKACHU that is in a weakened state.
When several of these POKéMON gather, their electricity could build and cause lightning storms.
Whenever PIKACHU comes across something new, it blasts it with a jolt of electricity. If you come across a blackened berry, it’s evidence that this POKéMON mistook the intensity of its charge.

This POKéMON has electricity-storing pouches on its cheeks. These appear to become electrically charged during the night while PIKACHU sleeps. It occasionally discharges electricity when it is dozy after waking up.
When Pikachu meet, they’ll touch their tails together and exchange electricity through them as a form of greeting.
It raises its tail to check its sur­ roundings. The tail is sometimes struck by light­ ning in this pose.
It raises its tail to check its surroundings. The tail is sometimes struck by lightning in this pose.
A plan was recently announced to gather many Pikachu and make an electric power plant.
Pikachu that can generate powerful electricity have cheek sacs that are extra soft and super stretchy.
While sleeping, it generates electricity in the sacs in its cheeks. If it’s not getting enough sleep, it will be able to use only weak electricity.
Its nature is to store up electricity. Forests where nests of Pikachu live are dangerous, since the trees are so often struck by lightning.
It occasionally uses an electric shock to recharge a fellow Pikachu that is in a weakened state.
It occasionally uses an electric shock to recharge a fellow Pikachu that is in a weakened state.
It raises its tail to check its surroundings. The tail is sometimes struck by lightning in this pose.
It has small electric sacs on both its cheeks. If threatened, it looses electric charges from the sacs.
It keeps its tail raised to monitor its surroundings. If you yank its tail, it will try to bite you.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: This Pokémon has electricity-storing pouches on its cheeks. These appear to become electrically charged during the night while Pikachu sleeps. It occasionally discharges electricity when it is dozy after waking up.

pokemon:raichu.name: Raichu
pokemon:raichu.summary: This Pokémon exudes a weak electrical charge from all over its body that makes it take on a slight glow in darkness. Raichu plants its tail in the ground to discharge electricity.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: This Pokémon exudes a weak electrical charge from all over its body that makes it take on a slight glow in darkness. Raichu plants its tail in the ground to discharge electricity.
Its tail discharges electricity into the ground, protecting it from getting shocked.
Its tail discharges electricity into the ground, protecting it from getting shocked.
Its long tail serves as a ground to protect itself from its own high voltage power.
If its electric pouches run empty, it raises its tail to gather electri­ city from the atmosphere.
It turns aggressive if it has too much electricity in its body. It discharges power through its tail.
If it stores too much electricity, its behavior turns aggressive. To avoid this, it occasionally discharges excess energy and calms itself down.
Its electric charges can reach even 100,000 volts. Careless contact can cause even an Indian elephant to faint.
When its electric­ ity builds, its muscles are stimu­ lated, and it be­ comes more aggres­ sive than usual.
When its electricity builds, its muscles are stimulated, and it becomes more aggressive than usual.
Its long tail serves as a ground to protect itself from its own high-voltage power.
It can discharge bursts of electricity exceeding 100,000 volts— a single strike with that amount of power would incapacitate one of the Copperajah of my homeland.
When electricity builds up inside its body, it becomes feisty. It also glows in the dark.
When electricity builds up inside its body, it becomes feisty. It also glows in the dark.
It becomes aggressive when it has electricity stored up. At such times, even its Trainer has to take care to avoid being attacked.
If the electrical sacs become excessively charged, Raichu plants its tail in the ground and discharges. Scorched patches of ground will be found near this Pokémon’s nest.
It can loose 100,000-volt bursts of electricity, instantly downing foes several times its size.
Its tail discharges electricity into the ground, protecting it from getting shocked.
Its long tail serves as a ground to protect itself from its own high voltage power.
If the electrical sacks become excessively charged, RAICHU plants its tail in the ground and discharges. Scorched patches of ground will be found near this POKéMON’s nest.
This POKéMON exudes a weak electrical charge from all over its body that makes it take on a slight glow in darkness. RAICHU searches for electricity by planting its tail in the ground.
If its electric pouches run empty, it raises its tail to gather electricity from the atmosphere.
If the electric pouches in its cheeks become fully charged, both ears will stand straight up.
If the electric pouches in its cheeks become fully charged, both ears will stand straight up.
It unleashes electric shocks that can reach 100,000 volts. When agitated, it can knock out even an Indian elephant.
Its long tail serves as a ground to protect itself from its own high-voltage power.
Because so many Trainers like the way Pikachu looks, you don’t see this Pokémon very often.
As electricity builds up inside its body, it becomes more aggressive. One theory is that the electricity buildup is actually causing stress.
Its tail discharges electricity into the ground, protecting it from getting shocked.
Its tail discharges electricity into the ground, protecting it from getting shocked.
When its electricity builds, its muscles are stimulated, and it becomes more aggressive than usual.
It can loose 100,000-volt bursts of electricity, instantly downing foes several times its size.
When electricity builds up inside its body, it becomes feisty. It also glows in the dark.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: This Pokémon exudes a weak electrical charge from all over its body that makes it take on a slight glow in darkness. Raichu plants its tail in the ground to discharge electricity.
